items.definition_en: A minute is a unit of time that is sixty seconds.
items.example_en: It takes five minutes to walk to the store.

items.definition_en: An hour is a unit of time equal to sixty minutes.
A second is a very short unit of time.
items.example_en: She waited for a few seconds.

items.definition_en: A day is a period of twenty-four hours.
A week is a period of seven days.
A month is one of the twelve parts of a year.
A year is a period of twelve months.
A moment is a very short period of time.
Morning is the time from sunrise until noon.
items.example_en: He eats breakfast in the morning.

items.definition_en: Evening is the time from sunset to bedtime.
items.example_en: They watch TV in the evening.

items.definition_en: Afternoon is the time between noon and evening.
items.example_en: We often drink coffee in the afternoon.

items.definition_en: Night is the time when it is dark and most people sleep.
Lunch time is the part of the day when people eat lunch.
items.example_en: We usually talk during lunch time.

items.definition_en: Bedtime is the usual time to go to bed.
A century is a period of one hundred years.
items.example_en: The building is two centuries old.

items.definition_en: A decade is a period of ten years.
items.example_en: He lived there for a decade.

items.definition_en: A holiday is a special day when people do not work.
Dawn is the time when the sun begins to rise.
Midnight is twelve o'clock at night.
A weekend is the time from Friday evening to Sunday night.
